term: yaṃ kiñci rūpaṃ ... vedanā ... saññā ... saṅkhārā ... viññāṇaṃ
literal_meaning: whatever form ... felt experience ... recognition ... putting-together activities ... knowing
preferred_translation: whatever form ... felt experience ... recognition ... putting-together activities ... knowing
alternative_translations: whatever form ... feeling ... perception ... formations ... consciousness
discouraged_translations: whatever body ... emotion ... concept ... volitional formations ... awareness
definition: Canonical pattern used to run the same analytical and not-self treatment across all five heaps.
notes: Use this record when the text is applying one coordinated formula across the five heaps. The phrase keeps the family in one voice and explicitly carries the heap-context override for saṅkhārā as putting-together activities. Comparative aggregate-language remains available for explanation, but direct translation should follow the house defaults of the coordinated family.

context_rules.context: In direct doctrinal translation of the canonical five-heap pattern
context_rules.rendering: whatever form ... felt experience ... recognition ... putting-together activities ... knowing

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose using familiar aggregate terminology
context_rules.rendering: whatever form ... feeling ... perception ... formations ... consciousness

example_phrases.pali: yaṃ kiñci rūpaṃ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṃ ... yaṃ kiñci viññāṇaṃ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṃ
example_phrases.translation: whatever form there is, past, future, or present ... whatever knowing there is, past, future, or present
example_phrases.notes: Canonical five-heap pattern used in the not-self discourse.
